PLAINTIFFS RESPONDENTS SHALL SUBMIT THEIR CONTROVERSY ALLEGING MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AND WRONGFUL DEATH ON THE PARTS OF JUDITH MATES, M.D., AS SETFORTH IN THEIS COMPLAINT, TO ARBITRATION ACCORDING TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT DATED AUG. 17, 2000 AND STAY OF THE PROCCEDINGS BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS-RESPONDENTS AND DEFENDANTS-PETITIONER IS ORDERED UNTIL THE CONTROVERSY THAT EXISTS BETWEEN SAID PARTIES HAS BEEN ARBITRATED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT DATED OCT. 8, 1997.THE COURT FINDS A PRE-EXITING RELATIONSHIP/CONNECTION TO THE SIGNATORY PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT.(SEE SCANNED DOCUMENTS FOR DETAILS)

Minor'S Compromise In Scd.
Off Calendar Nov-01-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Minor's Compromise hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Leticia Perez.
Randall Choy, Esq. for Petitioner: Kwan C. Der, present as Guardian at litem for and with: Tony Der, a minor Claimant, and with Cantonese Interpreter: Li Hong Tan.
Petitioner and Interpreter were sworn and examined.
On the basis of evidence received, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.
Attorney's Fees $468.75
Costs $73.88
A net balance of $1,332.37 to be deposited into a blocked account with United Commercial Bank, Clement Street, San Francisco, CA., no withdrawals shall be made without a written court order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.
Order Approving Compromise of Claim and Order to Deposit Money Into Blocked Account signed in open court.
